programs.name: Dance: Club Latin
programs.description: Have you always wanted to try Latin club dances like meringue, bachata and salsa? Then come learn basic steps, postures and holds through various exercises and engaging choreography. Partners are encouraged and helpful, but not necessary.

programs.name: Drawing and Painting
programs.description: Explore your creativity through pencils, pastels, acrylic paint and watercolours. Learn about and practice skills including sketching, shading, brush strokes, use of colour and texture.

programs.name: Family Fitness
programs.description: Do you like the idea of participating in physical activity with your family? Each week as you explore and try out various fitness activities, together as a family you will develop fitness fundamentals including balance, coordination and flexibility. The format of this class may vary from week to week. Each individual must register for this activity.

programs.name: Karate - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts.  Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Music: Keyboard
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to keyboarding. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.

programs.name: Music: Keyboard - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal instruction on the keyboard with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn proper hand positions, basic scales and how to read notes.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.

programs.name: Swim Lessons - Private
programs.description: Do you want to develop specific skills at your own pace? This activity provides personal swim instruction with a one-on-one ratio.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Volleyball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In volleyball, a team sport where the objective is to hit the ball so it makes contact with the opposing teams court, you will learn basics like serving, volleying, bumping, spiking and passing as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Dance: Hip Hop
programs.description: Hip Hop is a street dance that uses strong, fast and fluid movements. Learn basic steps like body rolls, contractions, bounces and waves through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Jazz
programs.description: This modern style of dance is all about fast footwork and lively movements. From kicks and lunges to leaps and gallops, learn proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Island Voyager
programs.description: Climb on board our ferries to be transported to the City's oasis, Toronto Island, and explore the challenges of being a voyager on the water. Here you will travel across the island, from the boathouse to the lagoons and the Gibraltar Lighthouse, to learn and practice canoeing, kayaking, portaging as well as fishing.  You will also learn about indigenous wildlife on the island and will take part in the arts through an environmental lens. Exploring activities on the different Islands will enable campers to hear stories and view curiosities. The ferry fee is included in the cost of the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Magic
programs.description: Magic tricks anyone? Through formal instruction, you will learn card manipulation, rope tricks, balloon sculpture and much more. Through practice and performance, you will build self-confidence and amaze your family and friends.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your magic skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Martial Arts - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts. Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Sewing
programs.description: Come get an introduction to this handy skill. Learn how to read a pattern, cut material, stich by hand or use a sewing machine with the aim of completing a simple project.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga. Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga/Pilates Fusion
programs.description: Yoga and Pilates movements are combined to create a unique fitness experience. Focusing on body alignment and overall flexibility, you will learn fundamentals from both practices to improve your strength, coordination and relaxation.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water one stroke at a time. Develop front crawl, back crawl, glides and staying afloat in deep water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 1.

programs.name: Adult Swim 3
programs.description: Build endurance in the water one stroke at a time. Develop staying afloat in water and stroke improvement. Work towards a continuous swim of 100 to 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 2.

programs.name: Cardio: High/Low
programs.description: Do you love aerobic exercise? With the ability to choose between high and low-impact movements, in this workout, you decide what works best for you to improve your overall fitness.

programs.name: Dance: Clogging
programs.description: This traditional European folk dance uses steel-plated shoes to create sound through movement. Learn proper posture and basic footwork like heel and toe striking, shuffles and jumps through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Pilates
programs.description: Build core strength and improve flexibility with this head-to-toe workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being.

programs.name: Science: Building Adventures with Caregiver
programs.description: Keep little hands busy with building. Stories, activities and fun and exciting experiments teach basic science knowledge and skills, while hands-on play using different materials and tools helps explore how to construct objects. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Zumba®
programs.description: Latin dance and music inspire this fun and energetic cardio workout. Using high and low-impact steps and aerobic movements, you will dance your way to new fitness heights.

programs.name: Computers: Smartphones
programs.description: Want to get savvy with your smartphone? Designed for users of various skill and competency levels, you will learn how to navigate and use your device, including texting and engaging on social media.Equipment: You must bring a smartphone.

programs.name: Introduction to Weaving
programs.description: Whether you're a complete beginner or looking  to refine your skills, this course is designed to guide you through the  fundamentals and creative possibilities of weaving. You'll learn the essentials  of weaving techniques, including loom setup, winding the warp, basic weave  structures, finishing techniques, terminology, loom descriptions and how to  read weaving patterns. Participants are welcome to bring their own yarns and  projects ideas. For  those who are new to weaving, beginner projects will be provided and yarn can  be purchased from the instructor, for a fee between $20-30.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Pilates
programs.description: Build core strength and improve flexibility with this head-to-toe workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being.

programs.name: Pilates With Baby
programs.description: Build core strength and improve flexibility with this head-to-toe workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being designed specifically for new caregivers. Here you can relax knowing participation is at your own pace and you can attend to your baby as needed. The recommended age for your baby is 1 to 12 months.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Musical Theatre
programs.description: Get ready to take the stage! Through a mixture of dance, drama and music instruction, campers will learn the art of performance.  You will sing, learn choreography and practice scenes to prepare for the end-of-session show.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your musical theatre skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Musical Theatre
programs.description: Get ready to take the stage! Through a mixture of dance, drama and music instruction, campers will learn the art of performance.  You will sing, learn choreography and practice scenes to prepare for the end-of-session show.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your musical theatre skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Racquet and Paddle
programs.description: Calling all racquet and paddle sports fans! This camp focuses on several racquet sports like tennis, pickleball and badminton. You will learn the fundamentals and work on developing skills such as the forehand, backhand and serving.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your racquet and paddle skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Science
programs.description: Are you ready to discover the world of science? Through fun-filled games, activities and hands-on experiments, you will investigate the concepts of science in this camp. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing your knowledge of science, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Island Voyager
programs.description: Climb on board our ferries to be transported to the City's oasis, Toronto Island, and explore the challenges of being a voyager on the water. Here you will travel across the island, from the boathouse to the lagoons and the Gibraltar Lighthouse, to learn and practice canoeing, kayaking, portaging as well as fishing.  You will also learn about indigenous wildlife on the island and will take part in the arts through an environmental lens. Exploring activities on the different Islands will enable campers to hear stories and view curiosities. The ferry fee is included in the cost of the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Magic

Through practice and performance, you will build self-confidence and amaze your family and friends.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your magic skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Musical Theatre
programs.description: Get ready to take the stage! Through a mixture of dance, drama and music instruction, campers will learn the art of performance.  You will sing, learn choreography and practice scenes to prepare for the end-of-session show.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your musical theatre skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

You will learn the fundamentals and work on developing skills such as the forehand, backhand and serving.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your racquet and paddle skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Science
programs.description: Are you ready to discover the world of science? Through fun-filled games, activities and hands-on experiments, you will investigate the concepts of science in this camp. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing your knowledge of science, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: Martial Arts - Beginner
programs.description: This activity introduces you to martial arts. Here you will develop fundamental martial art skills, simple sparring skills, tactical concepts, show willingness to engage in a variety of sport activities and have confidence to try new skills.

programs.name: Sewing
programs.description: Come get an introduction to this handy skill. Learn how to read a pattern, cut material, stich by hand or use a sewing machine with the aim of completing a simple project.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga. Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Yoga/Pilates Fusion
programs.description: Yoga and Pilates movements are combined to create a unique fitness experience. Focusing on body alignment and overall flexibility, you will learn fundamentals from both practices to improve your strength, coordination and relaxation.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water one stroke at a time. Develop front crawl, back crawl, glides and staying afloat in deep water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 1.

programs.name: Adult Swim 3
programs.description: Build endurance in the water one stroke at a time. Develop staying afloat in water and stroke improvement. Work towards a continuous swim of 100 to 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 2.

programs.name: Cardio: High/Low
programs.description: Do you love aerobic exercise? With the ability to choose between high and low-impact movements, in this workout, you decide what works best for you to improve your overall fitness.

programs.name: Dance: Clogging
programs.description: This traditional European folk dance uses steel-plated shoes to create sound through movement. Learn proper posture and basic footwork like heel and toe striking, shuffles and jumps through a variety of exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Pilates
programs.description: Build core strength and improve flexibility with this head-to-toe workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being.

programs.name: Science: Building Adventures with Caregiver
programs.description: Keep little hands busy with building. Stories, activities and fun and exciting experiments teach basic science knowledge and skills, while hands-on play using different materials and tools helps explore how to construct objects. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

programs.name: Zumba®
programs.description: Latin dance and music inspire this fun and energetic cardio workout. Using high and low-impact steps and aerobic movements, you will dance your way to new fitness heights.

programs.name: Computers: Smartphones
programs.description: Want to get savvy with your smartphone? Designed for users of various skill and competency levels, you will learn how to navigate and use your device, including texting and engaging on social media.Equipment: You must bring a smartphone.

programs.name: Introduction to Weaving
programs.description: Whether you're a complete beginner or looking  to refine your skills, this course is designed to guide you through the  fundamentals and creative possibilities of weaving. You'll learn the essentials  of weaving techniques, including loom setup, winding the warp, basic weave  structures, finishing techniques, terminology, loom descriptions and how to  read weaving patterns. Participants are welcome to bring their own yarns and  projects ideas. For  those who are new to weaving, beginner projects will be provided and yarn can  be purchased from the instructor, for a fee between $20-30.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Pickleball: Recreational
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Pilates
programs.description: Build core strength and improve flexibility with this head-to-toe workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being.

programs.name: Pilates With Baby
programs.description: Build core strength and improve flexibility with this head-to-toe workout. As you progress through slow-paced movements, performed in sequence, you will learn to incorporate an awareness of the spine and breath in this complete approach to well being designed specifically for new caregivers. Here you can relax knowing participation is at your own pace and you can attend to your baby as needed. The recommended age for your baby is 1 to 12 months.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Island Voyager
programs.description: Climb on board our ferries to be transported to the City's oasis, Toronto Island, and explore the challenges of being a voyager on the water. Here you will travel across the island, from the boathouse to the lagoons and the Gibraltar Lighthouse, to learn and practice canoeing, kayaking, portaging as well as fishing.  You will also learn about indigenous wildlife on the island and will take part in the arts through an environmental lens. Exploring activities on the different Islands will enable campers to hear stories and view curiosities. The ferry fee is included in the cost of the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Magic
programs.description: Magic tricks anyone? Through formal instruction, you will learn card manipulation, rope tricks, balloon sculpture and much more. Through practice and performance, you will build self-confidence and amaze your family and friends.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your magic skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Island Voyager
programs.description: Climb on board our ferries to be transported to the City's oasis, Toronto Island, and explore the challenges of being a voyager on the water. Here you will travel across the island, from the boathouse to the lagoons and the Gibraltar Lighthouse, to learn and practice canoeing, kayaking, portaging as well as fishing.  You will also learn about indigenous wildlife on the island and will take part in the arts through an environmental lens. Exploring activities on the different Islands will enable campers to hear stories and view curiosities. The ferry fee is included in the cost of the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Magic
programs.description: Magic tricks anyone? Through formal instruction, you will learn card manipulation, rope tricks, balloon sculpture and much more. Through practice and performance, you will build self-confidence and amaze your family and friends.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your magic skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Island Voyager
programs.description: Climb on board our ferries to be transported to the City's oasis, Toronto Island, and explore the challenges of being a voyager on the water. Here you will travel across the island, from the boathouse to the lagoons and the Gibraltar Lighthouse, to learn and practice canoeing, kayaking, portaging as well as fishing.  You will also learn about indigenous wildlife on the island and will take part in the arts through an environmental lens. Exploring activities on the different Islands will enable campers to hear stories and view curiosities. The ferry fee is included in the cost of the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Magic
programs.description: Magic tricks anyone? Through formal instruction, you will learn card manipulation, rope tricks, balloon sculpture and much more. Through practice and performance, you will build self-confidence and amaze your family and friends.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your magic skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Island Voyager
programs.description: Climb on board our ferries to be transported to the City's oasis, Toronto Island, and explore the challenges of being a voyager on the water. Here you will travel across the island, from the boathouse to the lagoons and the Gibraltar Lighthouse, to learn and practice canoeing, kayaking, portaging as well as fishing.  You will also learn about indigenous wildlife on the island and will take part in the arts through an environmental lens. Exploring activities on the different Islands will enable campers to hear stories and view curiosities. The ferry fee is included in the cost of the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Magic
programs.description: Magic tricks anyone? Through formal instruction, you will learn card manipulation, rope tricks, balloon sculpture and much more. Through practice and performance, you will build self-confidence and amaze your family and friends.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your magic skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.
